aiSummary.overview: The API security scan identified 16 failed findings across various security rules, indicating potential vulnerabilities that need addressing.

ruleGroups.name: API6 Unrestricted Access To Sensitive Business Flows
API3 Broken Object Property Level Authorization

findings.name: Missing anti-automation signal
findings.description: Business flow endpoints should expose anti-automation controls.
findings.impact: Weak anti-automation protections can enable scripted abuse, enumeration, and repeated business workflow execution.
findings.remediation: Use rate limits, bot controls, and abuse monitoring for sensitive flows.

ai.whyDangerous: Weak anti-automation protections can enable scripted abuse, enumeration, and repeated business workflow execution.
ai.remediation: Implement anti-automation controls such as rate limiting or CAPTCHA.
ai.businessImpact: Increased risk of abuse and potential service disruption.
ai.evidenceTitle: The endpoint did not present the expected anti-automation signal
ai.evidenceSummary: Sensitive business endpoint has no visible anti-automation/rate-limit signal.

evidence.reason: Sensitive business endpoint has no visible anti-automation/rate-limit signal.
evidence.title: The endpoint did not present the expected anti-automation signal
evidence.summary: Sensitive business endpoint has no visible anti-automation/rate-limit signal.

findings.name: Dev/staging hostname exposure
findings.description: Dev or staging hosts should not be exposed in production responses.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Dev/staging hostname exposure can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.

ai.whyDangerous: Dev/staging hostname exposure can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Ensure that production responses do not expose development or staging hostnames.
ai.businessImpact: Potential exposure of sensitive operations and data.
ai.evidenceTitle: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
ai.evidenceSummary: Response body regex check indicated potential exposure.

evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern

findings.name: Admin path without auth
findings.impact: If confirmed, Admin path without auth can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Protect administrative functions with server-side authorization checks.

ai.whyDangerous: Admin path without auth can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Require authentication for all admin-like paths.
ai.businessImpact: Risk of unauthorized access to sensitive operations.
ai.evidenceTitle: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response

evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Admin/function-level authorization probe returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Unsafe method override header
findings.description: Method override headers should not bypass authorization.
findings.impact: Method or function authorization gaps can let an attacker perform operations that should require higher privilege.
findings.remediation: Disable method override or apply authorization after effective method resolution.

ai.whyDangerous: Method or function authorization gaps can let an attacker perform operations that should require higher privilege.
ai.remediation: Ensure method override headers do not bypass authorization checks.
ai.businessImpact: Increased risk of unauthorized actions on sensitive endpoints.
ai.evidenceTitle: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
ai.evidenceSummary: Request with unsafe method override returned HTTP 500.

evidence.reason: Request with unsafe method override returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with unsafe method override returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Privileged query flag
findings.description: Client-controlled privileged flags should be rejected.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Privileged query flag can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Ignore privileged client-supplied flags and derive privileges from identity.

ai.whyDangerous: Privileged query flag can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Reject client-controlled privileged flags in requests.
ai.businessImpact: Potential unauthorized access to sensitive operations.
ai.evidenceTitle: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
ai.evidenceSummary: Request with privileged access tampering returned HTTP 500.

evidence.reason: Request with privileged access tampering 'privileged-query-flag' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with privileged access tampering 'privileged-query-flag' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Role header tampering
findings.description: Role headers should not grant privileges.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Role header tampering can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Do not trust client-controlled role headers.

ai.whyDangerous: Role header tampering can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Do not allow role headers to grant privileges.
ai.businessImpact: Risk of unauthorized access to sensitive operations.
ai.evidenceTitle: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
ai.evidenceSummary: Request with privileged access tampering returned HTTP 500.

evidence.reason: Request with privileged access tampering 'role-header-admin' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with privileged access tampering 'role-header-admin' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Missing rate-limit headers
findings.description: Sensitive APIs should expose standard or de-facto rate-limit signals.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Missing rate-limit headers can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Implement and communicate rate limits using standard RateLimit headers or documented compatibility headers.

ai.whyDangerous: Missing rate-limit headers can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Implement standard rate-limit headers in API responses.
ai.businessImpact: Increased risk of abuse and potential service disruption.
ai.evidenceTitle: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
ai.evidenceSummary: Baseline response does not expose common rate-limit headers.

evidence.reason: Baseline response does not expose common rate-limit headers.
evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
evidence.summary: Baseline response does not expose common rate-limit headers.

findings.name: Internal/debug fields exposed
findings.description: Internal fields should not be exposed.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Internal/debug fields exposed can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Filter internal and debug fields from serialized responses.

ai.whyDangerous: Internal/debug fields exposed can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
ai.remediation: Ensure internal fields are not exposed in API responses.
ai.businessImpact: Potential exposure of sensitive operations and data.
ai.evidenceTitle: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
ai.evidenceSummary: Response body regex check indicated potential exposure.

evidence.title: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior

findings.name: Mass assignment role field
findings.description: Role fields should not be accepted from clients.
findings.impact: Unexpected writable fields can let an attacker change protected object properties that should be controlled by the server.
findings.remediation: Use explicit input DTOs and deny-list privileged fields.

ai.whyDangerous: Unexpected writable fields can let an attacker change protected object properties that should be controlled by the server.
ai.remediation: Do not accept role fields from clients.
ai.businessImpact: Risk of unauthorized changes to protected properties.
ai.evidenceTitle: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
ai.evidenceSummary: Request with unexpected field mutation returned HTTP 500.

evidence.reason: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-role-field' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
evidence.summary: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-role-field' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Mass assignment ownership field
findings.description: Ownership fields should not be accepted from clients.
findings.impact: Unauthorized writable fields can expose or modify attributes outside the intended object ownership boundary.
findings.remediation: Bind ownership on the server from authenticated identity.

ai.whyDangerous: Unauthorized writable fields can expose or modify attributes outside the intended object ownership boundary.
ai.remediation: Do not accept ownership fields from clients.
ai.businessImpact: Risk of unauthorized access to sensitive attributes.
ai.evidenceTitle: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
ai.evidenceSummary: Request with unexpected field mutation returned HTTP 500.

evidence.reason: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-owner-field' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
evidence.summary: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-owner-field' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Read-only field mutation
findings.description: Read-only fields should not be writable.
findings.impact: Relaxed object property validation can let an attacker tamper with fields that influence business decisions.
findings.remediation: Ignore or reject read-only client-supplied fields.

evidence.reason: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-readonly-field' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
evidence.summary: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-readonly-field' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Unknown JSON property accepted
findings.description: Unknown JSON fields should be rejected or ignored safely.
findings.impact: Improper property-level authorization can expose hidden fields or allow unauthorized updates.
findings.remediation: Reject unexpected JSON properties for sensitive endpoints.

evidence.reason: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-unknown-json-property' returned HTTP 500.
evidence.title: Unexpected field mutation changed server behavior
evidence.summary: Request with unexpected field mutation 'add-unknown-json-property' returned HTTP 500. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Endpoint should reject missing Authorization header
findings.description: Authenticated endpoints should reject missing authorization.
findings.impact: An attacker may access protected functionality without valid authentication by removing an existing credential.

evidence.reason: Endpoint returned HTTP 500 after auth credential was removed.
evidence.title: Endpoint accepted request after authentication was removed
evidence.summary: Endpoint returned HTTP 500 after auth credential was removed. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Missing auth should not return same body as baseline
findings.description: Removing auth should not return the same sensitive body.
findings.impact: A missing credential boundary can allow a caller to continue using a session or token after it should have been rejected.
findings.remediation: Return an auth error body instead of protected resource data.

evidence.reason: Endpoint returned HTTP 500 after auth credential was removed.
evidence.title: Endpoint accepted request after authentication was removed
evidence.summary: Endpoint returned HTTP 500 after auth credential was removed. Baseline status was 500 and mutated status was 500.

findings.name: Server version disclosure
findings.description: Server version headers should not disclose stack details.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Server version disclosure can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.

evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern

findings.name: Missing anti-sniffing security header
findings.description: APIs should prevent MIME sniffing where browser clients may consume responses.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Missing anti-sniffing security header can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on API responses. Use cache-control rules separately for sensitive data.

evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
